_comment: Canonical error response shapes for JSON APIs. See ai/rules/API_CONVENTION.md. Every error has the same top-level structure: { error: { code, message, ... } }. The 'code' is a stable machine-readable string clients can switch on. The 'message' is human-readable but English; localize on the client.

validation_error_422._when: POST/PATCH with invalid input. Use 400 if the input is malformed (not parseable). Use 422 if the input is well-formed but semantically invalid.

bad_request_400._when: Malformed JSON, missing required header, unsupported content type.

error.message: Request body is not valid JSON.

unauthenticated_401._when: No auth credentials provided, or they're invalid/expired.

forbidden_403._when: Authenticated but not allowed for this resource/action.

error.message: You do not have permission to perform this action.

not_found_404._when: Resource does not exist OR the requester doesn't own it. Always return 404 instead of 403 for ownership mismatches to avoid leaking existence.

method_not_allowed_405._when: Endpoint doesn't support this HTTP method.

conflict_409._when: Request conflicts with current state (duplicate, version mismatch, business rule violation).

error.message: Too many requests. Retry after 60 seconds.

server_error_500._when: Unhandled exception. Never include stack trace or internal details. Always include a request_id so support can trace logs.

error.message: Something went wrong. Please try again later.

service_unavailable_503._when: Dependency (DB, broker, third-party) is down. Use during maintenance or degraded operation.

error.message: Service is temporarily unavailable. Please try again in a few minutes.

_principles: Always wrap in { error: { ... } } — never bare error fields at top level
code is stable; clients switch on it
message is human-readable English; localize on client
Don't leak internal IDs, stack traces, schema, or query details
Include request_id on 5xx so support can correlate to logs
Return 404 (not 403) for ownership mismatches to avoid leaking existence
